name: La Vía Verde de la Vall Fosca — Ruta circular
region_intro: Mountain biking around Embassament de Sallente features challenging routes through a mountainous landscape. The region is characterized by significant elevation changes, high mountain passes, and deep valleys, often with views of the reservoir. Terrain includes a mix of technical singletrack and wider paths, suitable for experienced riders. The area's geology contributes to varied riding surfaces, from rocky ascents to forested descents. Best mountain bike trails around Embassament de Sallente The most popular mountain bike trail is La Vía Verde de la Vall Fosca — Ruta circular, a 18.8 miles (30.3 km) difficult trail that takes 3 hours 36 minutes to complete. This route features substantial climbs and descents through the Vall Fosca. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Trail from Sentís to Sas – Pista d'Erta loop from Estany Gento, a difficult 73.7 miles (118.7 km) path. This extensive route covers diverse terrain with significant elevation gain. Local mountain bikers also love the Climb to Collado del Triador – Triador Pass and Viewpoint loop from la Central de Cabdella, a 36.8 miles (59.3 km) trail leading through mountain passes and offering panoramic viewpoints, often completed in about 6 hours 38 minutes. Mountain biking around Embassament de Sallente is defined by high mountain passes, deep valleys, and challenging climbs. The network offers options for moderate to difficult ability levels. The routes in Embassament de Sallente are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from 8 reviews. More than 230 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Embassament de Sallente's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Embassament de Sallente?
A: There are &lt;strong&gt;8 mountain bike trails&lt;/strong&gt; documented on komoot for the Embassament de Sallente area. These routes primarily cater to experienced riders, featuring significant elevation changes and varied terrain.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners?
A: The mountain bike trails around Embassament de Sallente are generally challenging, with 6 out of 8 routes rated as 'difficult'. There are &lt;strong&gt;2 moderate routes&lt;/strong&gt; available, but beginners should be prepared for mountainous terrain and elevation changes. The region is characterized by high mountain passes and deep valleys.

Q: What are the typical lengths of mountain bike trails in this area?
A: Trail lengths vary significantly. You can find routes ranging from approximately 26 km (16 miles) to over 118 km (73 miles). For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17548064" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Triador Pass and Viewpoint – Climb to Collado del Triador loop from la Central de Cabdella&lt;/a&gt; is about 26 km, while the extensive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38163376" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail from Sentís to Sas – Pista d'Erta loop from Estany Gento&lt;/a&gt; covers nearly 119 km.

Q: When is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Embassament de Sallente?
A: The best time for mountain biking in Embassament de Sallente is typically from &lt;strong&gt;late spring to early autumn&lt;/strong&gt; (June to October). During these months, the weather is generally more stable, and the high mountain passes are usually free of snow. Always check local weather forecasts and trail conditions before heading out, as mountain weather can change rapidly.

Q: What kind of challenges can advanced mountain bikers expect around Embassament de Sallente?
A: Advanced mountain bikers will find plenty of challenges, including &lt;strong&gt;significant elevation gains and descents&lt;/strong&gt;, technical singletrack, and rocky ascents. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10467408" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Climb to Collado del Triador – Triador Pass and Viewpoint loop from la Central de Cabdella&lt;/a&gt; involve nearly 2,000 meters of climbing, traversing mountain passes and offering panoramic views.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in the area?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes around Embassament de Sallente are circular. A popular option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e957188667/la-via-verde-de-la-vall-fosca-ruta-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Vía Verde de la Vall Fosca — Ruta circular&lt;/a&gt;, which is a difficult 30 km loop featuring substantial climbs and descents through the Vall Fosca.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy the most about mountain biking in Embassament de Sallente?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars. Reviewers often praise the &lt;strong&gt;challenging terrain, the stunning mountainous landscapes, and the rewarding views&lt;/strong&gt;, especially from high passes and viewpoints overlooking the reservoir.

Q: Where can I find parking for mountain biking near Embassament de Sallente?
A: Common starting points for many routes, such as &lt;strong&gt;la Central de Cabdella&lt;/strong&gt; and &lt;strong&gt;Estany Gento&lt;/strong&gt;, typically offer parking facilities. It's advisable to check specific route descriptions for detailed parking information, as options can vary.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or scenic spots along the mountain bike trails?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its panoramic vistas. Many routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6954854" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Triador Pass and Viewpoint – Estany Gento loop from la Central de Cabdella&lt;/a&gt;, lead through mountain passes and offer spectacular viewpoints of the surrounding valleys and the Embassament de Sallente itself.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the mountain bike trails?
A: The terrain is diverse, ranging from &lt;strong&gt;technical singletrack to wider paths&lt;/strong&gt;. You'll encounter rocky ascents, forested descents, and varied surfaces influenced by the area's geology. Riders should be prepared for a mix of challenging and rewarding surfaces.

Q: Are there any moderate mountain bike routes for those looking for less extreme challenges?
A: While most routes are difficult, there are &lt;strong&gt;2 moderate mountain bike routes&lt;/strong&gt; available. These still involve significant elevation changes typical of the mountainous region but are less demanding than the most challenging trails. Always review the route details to ensure it matches your skill level.
tips: Mythical hill and viewpoint of the Vall Fosca that I recommend to all lovers of sports and/or nature. Beautiful views of the Pyrenees, the Aigüestortes National Park, the Vall d'Assua, the Pic de l'Orri and the Vall Fosca.

In an emblematic place such as the Vall Fosca, at an altitude of 2,200 meters, we will cross an old train track, currently in disuse, and we will cross its tunnels. In few places in the world can we find a train track at such a height.
If we go with children, I recommend taking a small flashlight for the tunnels, some are a little longer or have the occasional puddle.

Another of the tunnels that we found during our passage through the Vall Fosca greenway. An emblematic place, at 2200 meters of altitude, we will cross an old train track, currently in disuse, and we will cross its tunnels. In few places in the world can we find a train track at such a height!
If we go with children, I recommend taking a small flashlight to cross the tunnels, some are a little longer or have the occasional puddle.

As we pass through the Vía Verde de la Vall Fosca, we come across this lake of glacial origin, located in the Vall Fosca, at 2,140 m above sea level.
There is an information point nearby.
Easy access is from the Vall Fosca cable car (tickets must be purchased online)

Go up or down 450 meters of altitude in 14 minutes through the Vall Fosca, but in a different way.
This is the Cable Car, which bears the name of the valley and takes us up to more than 2000 meters, next to Lake Gento.
Starting point of many mountain routes and passage of great itineraries.
We can buy tickets online or from there. There is a bar/restaurant service upstairs.
